findings.description: Repeated header/footer content inserted between page-equivalent sections in the HTML disrupts reading order. Officials' names (PAT McCRORY/Governor, DONALD R. VAN DER VAART/Secretary, TRACY DAVIS/Director), footer address blocks, and colored divider bars appear inline between content sections throughout the continuous HTML document.
findings.evidence: In HTML chunk 1, the officials' names appear twice: first at the top (matching PDF letterhead position) and again after a footer block and color bar, before the MEMORANDUM heading. Similar footer text and color bars separate content corresponding to PDF page boundaries across all HTML chunks.

findings.description: A full-width page-break artifact image (likely a footer bar/stripe from the PDF page boundary) is inserted mid-content between the document outline and Section I, breaking reading flow. This is a PDF page element that should not appear in the HTML body.
findings.evidence:
HTML contains between the outline
    and the

    I. History/Background

    heading. This image does not correspond to any substantive content in the PDF; it is a page-transition artifact.
